title: Mermaid Document Diagrams Delivery
summary: Add repository-built Mermaid source intake, targeted and full rendering, watcher integration, rendered-info source links, and two proof diagrams.
content_html:
Mermaid Document Diagrams Delivery

Outcome

A Docs Viewer author can add a staged Mermaid source through Add image…, retain the canonical .mmd inside the active scope, and receive a verified SVG token in the current Markdown buffer. In rendered manage mode, Document Info lists the canonical Mermaid sources behind the SVGs being viewed and opens them in VS Code. Repository authors, targeted document builds, full builds, and Docs Watcher use the same registered producer. Readers receive only sanitized static SVG.

The delivery is complete when two useful Studio documents visibly prove the convention:

  • Configuration And Extension Points receives a diagram of source configuration, executable ownership, generated projection, and runtime capability.
  • Docs Import receives a diagram of document intake, embedded media, source-editor media, publication, source write, and rebuild boundaries.

Delivery Boundary

In scope:

  • one focused, locked Mermaid CLI dependency beneath docs-viewer/build/mermaid/;
  • one registered mermaid source-media producer publishing same-basename SVG through the configured svg class;
  • Studio media/mermaid/ source registration and visible source folder;
  • staged .mmd intake through Add image…, followed by publication verification and token insertion;
  • complete rendering during a full Docs build and relevant-identity rendering during targeted builds;
  • changed-identity rendering when Docs Watcher observes a configured .mmd source;
  • manage-only Mermaid source links in rendered Document Info, delegating scope-confined identities to VS Code;
  • shared sanitization, accessibility metadata, focused service/build tests, and the two proof diagrams.

Out of scope:

  • Mermaid parsing, editing, or live preview inside Docs Viewer;
  • a graphical diagram editor or browser Mermaid runtime;
  • global mmdc, an alternative Docker renderer, or multiple producer choices;
  • fingerprints, render caches, incremental dependency graphs, or background queues;
  • automatic sharing of one .mmd source across scopes;
  • migration of historical SVG or inline diagrams;
  • D2 support unless a proof diagram exposes a concrete Mermaid limitation.

Settled Decisions

  • Pin @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli exactly to 11.16.0 in docs-viewer/build/mermaid/package.json and commit the adjacent package-lock.json.
  • Keep shared Mermaid settings in docs-viewer/build/mermaid/mermaid-config.json: neutral theme, strict security, and htmlLabels: false.
  • Render SVG on a white background with an explicit 1200 by 800 browser viewport, retain a responsive viewBox, and add no Markdown dimensions by default.
  • Require Mermaid accTitle and accDescr plus useful Markdown alt text.
  • A full build renders every configured Mermaid source. Targeted builds, staged intake, and the watcher render only relevant identities.
  • Rendered Document Info lists Mermaid sources derived from the saved document's SVG tokens, configured producer relationship, and existing same-basename .mmd files. Source mode remains the semantic-token picker.
  • VS Code is the initial external source editor; no separate diagram toolbar action is added.

Delivery Tracker

Current state: planned and not active.

Next checkpoint: DIA-1 — establish the pinned toolchain and one provider-neutral producer.

[ ] DIA-1 — Toolchain And Producer

  • [ ] Add the focused package manifest, exact dependency, lockfile, render config, and ignored local node_modules/ boundary.
  • [ ] Add one Python-owned producer that invokes the repository-local executable, renders into temporary output, sanitizes the SVG, and publishes through the configured location adapter.
  • [ ] Fail explicitly for a missing installation, invalid Mermaid source, renderer failure, sanitizer removal that invalidates output, or failed publication verification.
  • [ ] Prove deterministic same-basename identities and no writes during dry-run planning.

[ ] DIA-2 — Scope And Build Integration

  • [ ] Register Studio source.build_media.mermaid at media/mermaid/, targeting published svg, and materialize the source folder.
  • [ ] Run every configured Mermaid identity before document rendering in a full build.
  • [ ] Resolve and render only Mermaid SVG identities referenced by --only-doc-ids before rendering those document payloads.
  • [ ] Keep search rebuilding dependent on document prose changes, not ordinary same-token diagram edits.

[ ] DIA-3 — Author And Watcher Workflows

  • [ ] Extend Add image… to list staged .mmd alongside finished image formats and route it through canonical-source copy, producer execution, publication verification, and token insertion.
  • [ ] Leave the active Markdown buffer dirty for Rebuild doc and retain the staged source after success.
  • [ ] Add a manage-only Diagram sources section to rendered Document Info, listing each verified Mermaid-backed SVG as an Open in VS Code link without exposing physical paths.
  • [ ] Keep Markdown source-mode Document Info exclusively on the existing semantic-token picker; add no diagram toolbar action or caret-based inference.
  • [ ] Extend Docs Watcher to recognize configured Mermaid source and render only the changed identity.

[ ] DIA-4 — Proof Documents And Closeout

  • [ ] Author one accessible, scope-owned .mmd and insert its SVG token into Configuration And Extension Points.
  • [ ] Author one accessible, scope-owned .mmd and insert its SVG token into Docs Import.
  • [ ] Verify both diagrams in rendered manage mode, responsive layout, and dark mode; leave tactile visual judgment to manual review.
  • [ ] Run focused producer, build, route/service, watcher, config, sanitizer, and generated-contract checks.
  • [ ] Promote stable workflow and extension rules to the durable owners, update the feature parent and roadmap, and close this delivery only when both proof diagrams ship.

Done When

  • canonical .mmd source and derived .svg have one scope-owned, reproducible relationship;
  • interactive intake, repository authoring, targeted build, full build, and watcher execution use the same producer;
  • no token is inserted and no document source is written after render or publication failure;
  • generated SVG survives sanitization, includes accessibility metadata, and renders correctly without a browser Mermaid dependency;
  • both proof documents contain useful diagrams rather than placeholder examples;
  • Mermaid remains a build dependency, not a reader or source-editor runtime dependency.
